title: CapabilityToken
description: An immutable, unforgeable capability token for the NEXUS microkernel. A CapabilityToken simultaneously names a kernel object and encodes the permitted operations on it. Tokens are minted only by NexusKernel and can never be constructed by callers. Modelled after seL4 capability derivation (seL4 Reference Manual v15.0.0).

token_id.description: UUID4 unique identifier for this capability token instance.

object_id.description: UUID4 identifier of the kernel object this token grants access to (process, channel, memory region, device, etc.).

object_type.description: Type of kernel object referenced by object_id. Aligned with seL4 kernel object types.

permitted_ops.description: Exhaustive list of operations this token permits on the named object (e.g., ['read', 'write'], ['invoke'], ['grant', 'revoke']). A token NEVER grants operations beyond this list.

issuer.description: Identifier of the NexusKernel instance that minted this token. Only NexusKernel may issue tokens.

holder.description: Optional identifier of the process or agent currently holding this token.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of token creation.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC expiry timestamp. Null means the token does not expire (persistent capability).

parent_token_id.description: Optional UUID4 of the parent token from which this token was derived. Supports seL4-style capability derivation trees. A derived token can only have a subset of the parent's permitted_ops.

revoked.description: True if this token has been explicitly revoked by NexusKernel. Revoked tokens MUST be rejected by all kernel object handlers.

audit_tags.description: Optional audit tags for governance tracking and security review.
